### Post by foobar on 2005-08-13

i am still unable to get my tv (via s-video) to work as a second monitor.

### Post by foobar on 2005-08-14
is it possible to use a 2nd display with a s-cable?

---

### Post by kokiri on 2005-08-14
I have found that the easiest way to get tv-out with an nvidia card is to use the nv-tv-out software that can be found here: [Nv-TV-Out Sourceforge Home](http://sourceforge.net/projects/nv-tv-out) 

That way it works as intended with hotkey switching and all that fancy jazz like in win.

---

### Post by srikanthkv on 2007-12-09
You may try this too. I am able to get the S-video output to TV successfully.   xrandr --output S-video --set load_detection 1    gets your TV enabled. and xrandr --output S-video --mode 800x600 sets your S-video output to the TV. Please refer to man xrandr for more details. You may have to add a mode to the TV display. xrandr --addmode S-video 800x600 does the needful in this case.

---
